title: George parkrun celebrates a decade of forest fun
bodyText:
Video

GEORGE NEWS & VIDEO - Parkrunners are showing their commitment this morning by leaving their warm beds to participate in a 5km run or walk through the pine forest at the back of the Garden Route Botanical Gardens in Caledon Street.

Although many athletes wouldn't miss a parkrun for anything in the world, others decided to pitch for mere celebratory reasons.


> Today, 6 April, is no ordinary day - it is George parkrun's tenth birthday.


Media liaison officer Wayne Ryder and the rest of the management are very excited to celebrate a decade of hard work and persistence to get our town moving.

"The weather had us a bit worried at one point, but we decided to persist. It is after all our parkrun's birthday," he said.

Many runners are clad in blue - the symbolic colour in parkrun lingo for having completed 500 parkruns.


> VIDEO: George parkrun's 10th birthday celebrations


Your browser does not support HTML5 video.

Among the participants is 85-year-old Fransie Jansen, also known as Speed Queen, who will undertake her 221st parkrun today.

Read more about Fransie in an article here: Fantastiese Fransie vol lus vir die lewe

Photos: Michelle Pienaar‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
